output_debit_classifier.reason: This transaction involves the repurchase and immediate cancellation of the company's own shares. The transaction text explicitly states the shares were "immediately cancelled," which means this is not a treasury stock transaction but rather a permanent retirement of shares. Under IFRS, when shares are cancelled: (1) the Common Shares account is reduced by the par value of the cancelled shares (10,000 shares × $5.00 = $50,000); (2) the excess of the repurchase price over par value is charged to Retained Earnings (10,000 shares × ($6.00 - $5.00) = $10,000); and (3) Cash is reduced by the total amount paid (10,000 shares × $6.00 = $60,000). The entry balances: debits $60,000 = credits $60,000.

output_entry_builder.reason: Eagle Bulk Shipping repurchased and cancelled 10,000 common shares. The par value ($50,000) is removed from Common Shares. The excess paid over par ($10,000) reduces Retained Earnings. Cash decreases by the total purchase price ($60,000). Under IFRS, share cancellations are recorded by removing the par value from share capital and charging any excess to retained earnings.
